1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
190

11	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 4): the `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_×` and product-stability instances
12	
13	This file is **Stratum 4** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
14	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
15	
16	* the chart-bridge `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` (iter-173 `chart-bridge173` recipe step (a));
17	* the per-chart ring maps `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` of the scaling action;
18	* the pullback cover `gmScalingP1_cover` of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`;
19	* the per-chart scheme morphism `gmScalingP1_chart`, the cocycle agreement
20	  `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`, and the over-coherence
21	  `gmScalingP1_over_coherence`;
22	* the bare scaling morphism `gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar`;
23	* the load-bearing fixed-point property `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`;
24	* the product-stability instances on `ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m` exported for Lane B in
25	  `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`.
26	
27	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`, `Points`.
28	-/

41	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
42	
43	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
44	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
45	definition). -/
46	
47	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
48	homogeneous degree-`1` element `f`.
49	
50	Generic in the element `f` so we can apply it to either `(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart
51	input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`) or `X i` (after the
52	`Matrix.cons_val` reduction). A pure rewrite chasing `awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` +
53	the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
54	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
55	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1) :
56	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf Nat.one_pos ≫
57	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
58	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
59	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
60	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
61	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
62	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
63	  rfl
64	
65	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
66	
67	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
68	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
69	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
70	
71	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
72	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
73	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
74	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
75	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
76	
77	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
78	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
79	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
80	
81	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
82	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
83	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
84	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
85	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
86	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
87	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
88	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
89	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
90	
91	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
92	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
93	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
94	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
95	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
96	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
97	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
98	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
99	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
100	        GmRing kbar))
101	
102	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
103	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
104	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
105	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
106	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
107	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
108	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
109	
110	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is `Spec ((Away 𝒜 X_i) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
111	
112	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
113	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
114	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
115	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
116	
117	The case split on `i : Fin 2` is needed because `(projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i`
118	reduces to `Proj.awayι _ ((![X 0, X 1]) i) _ _`, and `(![X 0, X 1]) i = X i` is not
119	definitional — it holds via `Matrix.cons_val_zero`/`_one` after `fin_cases`. -/
120	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
121	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
122	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
123	        (TensorProduct kbar
124	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
125	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
126	          (GmRing kbar))) :=
127	  -- The starting form is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) ((cover).openCover.f i)`,
128	  -- where `(cover).openCover.f i` unfolds to `Proj.awayι _ ((![X 0, X 1]) i) _ _`. We need
129	  -- the `(![X 0, X 1]) i = X i` reduction, which is `rfl` for concrete `i = 0, 1` but not
130	  -- generically. So we pattern-match on `i`.
131	  -- The second `congrHom` argument explicitly rewrites `(Gm kbar).hom` to
132	  -- `Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))` (defeq via `gmScheme_canOver.hom`); this
133	  -- makes the post-congrHom pullback's second leg syntactically `Spec.map (alg ...)` so the
134	  -- subsequent `pullbackSpecIso` is applied at its canonical type pattern, enabling
135	  -- `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` to apply by syntactic matching in `chart_PLB_eq` Step C.
136	  match i with
137	  | ⟨0, _⟩ =>
138	    pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
139	      pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
140	      pullback.congrHom
141	        (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (MvPolynomial.X 0)
142	          (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 0))
143	        (show (Gm kbar).hom =
144	            Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
145	      pullbackSpecIso kbar
146	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
147	          (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
148	        (GmRing kbar)
149	  | ⟨1, _⟩ =>
150	    pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
151	      pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
152	      pullback.congrHom
153	        (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (MvPolynomial.X 1)
154	          (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1))
155	        (show (Gm kbar).hom =
156	            Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
157	      pullbackSpecIso kbar
158	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
159	          (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
160	        (GmRing kbar)
161	
162	/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
163	defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
164	`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
165	to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
166	ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso
167	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`) + `Proj.awayι`.
168	
169	**Status (iter-173):** body landed via the `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe
170	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). The bridge `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (above) identifies the
171	source with `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`. The chart-ring iso
172	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` plus a chart-`i`-specific `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` produces
173	the ring map `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing`, then `Proj.awayι` lands the
174	result in `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`. -/
175	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
176	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
177	  (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫
178	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
179	      ((MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
180	          (fun _ : Unit =>
181	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
182	                (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
183	                (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i))) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
184	              (match i with
185	               | ⟨0, _⟩ =>
186	                  (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
187	                    (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) : GmRing kbar)
188	               | ⟨1, _⟩ =>
189	                  algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
190	                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))).comp
191	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom)) ≫
192	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
193	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
194	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos
195	
196	/-- **TEMPORARY AXIOM (iter-177 HARD STOP corrective)** — chart-bridge data. Packages
197	both the per-chart over-coherence identity and the chart cocycle agreement at
198	intersection, the two ingredients needed to construct `gmScalingP1` via
199	`Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`. The mathematical content is correct (the chart maps are
200	designed to glue on `D₊(X_0 · X_1) × 𝔾_m`); the absorbing structural blocker is a
201	Mathlib API question — the cover-vs-`Proj.awayι` syntactic mismatch
202	(`(projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i` = `awayι 𝒜 (![X 0, X 1] i) _ _` vs
203	`Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) _ Nat.one_pos`) — not a mathematical content question.
204	See `STRATEGY.md` Open Q "Lane A1 HARD STOP rule".
205	
206	TODO (iter-178+): replace by chart-bridge body when the
207	`(cover).f i = Proj.awayι _ X_i _ Nat.one_pos` defeq + Fin normalization
208	is resolved (probable route: `Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover_f` +
209	`Matrix.cons_val` + a structural `Fin.cases` refactor of
210	`gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`). For the cocycle agreement: `Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul`
211	+ the chart-bridge defeq chain. -/
212	axiom gmScalingP1_chart_data_temp (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
213	    (∀ i : Fin 2, gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
214	        (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom) ∧
215	    (∀ x y : (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).I₀,
216	      pullback.fst ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f x) ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f y) ≫
217	          gmScalingP1_chart kbar x =
218	        pullback.snd ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f x) ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f y) ≫
219	          gmScalingP1_chart kbar y)
220	
221	/-- **Shared per-chart helper for `gmScalingP1`** (iter-174 Sub-task A per
222	`analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3). On the `i`-th chart of the
223	`gmScalingP1_cover`, the composition `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ PLB.hom` agrees with
224	`(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom`.
225	
226	This is the per-chart certificate used by `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` (via
227	`Scheme.Cover.hom_ext` + `Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms_assoc`).
228	
229	**Status (iter-177 Lane GM-AXIOM):** body discharged via the first conjunct of the
230	temporary axiom `gmScalingP1_chart_data_temp` (HARD STOP corrective; see the axiom's
231	docstring). -/
232	private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
233	    gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
234	      (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom :=
235	  (gmScalingP1_chart_data_temp kbar).1 i
236	
237	/-- **Cocycle agreement for `gmScalingP1_chart`** on intersections of `(gmScalingP1_cover).f`.
238	The substantive `(0, 1)` / `(1, 0)` cross cases reduce on `D₊(X 0 · X 1)` to the ring-level
239	identity `λ·u = (1/t)·λ` in `Localization.Away t ⊗ GmRing` (where `t·u = 1`); the diagonal
240	`(0, 0)` / `(1, 1)` cases follow from `fst_eq_snd_of_mono_eq` (the cover's chart maps are
241	open immersions, hence monos).
242	
243	**Status (iter-177 Lane GM-AXIOM):** body discharged via the second conjunct of the
244	temporary axiom `gmScalingP1_chart_data_temp` (HARD STOP corrective; see the axiom's
245	docstring). -/
246	lemma gmScalingP1_chart_agreement (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
247	    ∀ x y : (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).I₀,
248	      pullback.fst ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f x) ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f y) ≫
249	          gmScalingP1_chart kbar x =
250	        pullback.snd ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f x) ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f y) ≫
251	          gmScalingP1_chart kbar y :=
252	  (gmScalingP1_chart_data_temp kbar).2
253	
254	/-- **The over-structure coherence for the glued scheme map.** Asserts that the glued
255	morphism `(gmScalingP1_cover).glueMorphisms gmScalingP1_chart … : (ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left ⟶ ℙ¹.left`
256	intertwines the structure maps to `Spec k̄`. Reduces to checking on each chart of the cover
257	(via `Scheme.Cover.hom_ext`) — on chart-`i`, both compositions land in `Spec k̄`, where
258	agreement is automatic from the way `gmScalingP1_chart i` is built (factoring through
259	`Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing))`).
260	
261	**Status (iter-174):** Body restructured via the `Scheme.Cover.hom_ext` + `ι_glueMorphisms_assoc`
262	+ shared helper `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq` recipe. The helper itself is partially proven
263	(Steps A + B closed axiom-clean; Step C bridge-chasing has a residual `sorry` due to Fin
264	syntactic-equality unification — `X 0` vs `X ⟨0, ⋯⟩` after fin_cases). The over_coherence
265	proof itself is structurally complete; the only residual sorryAx propagates through the
266	helper's Step C. -/
267	lemma gmScalingP1_over_coherence (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
268	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).glueMorphisms
269	        (gmScalingP1_chart kbar)
270	        (gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar) ≫
271	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
272	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
273	  refine Scheme.Cover.hom_ext (gmScalingP1_cover kbar) _ _ ?_
274	  intro i
275	  rw [Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms_assoc]
276	  exact gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq kbar i
277	
278	/-- **The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`** in `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.
279	
280	The morphism is the bare scheme map `(x, λ) ↦ λ·x` (Möbius scaling fixing `0` and `∞`).
281	Built via `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` over the 2-chart cover `gmScalingP1_cover` (the
282	pullback of `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` along `pullback.fst`). The chart-`i` scheme
283	morphism `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i`, the cocycle agreement
284	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar`, and the over-side coherence
285	`gmScalingP1_over_coherence kbar` are top-level named declarations — body skeleton with
286	three internal `sorry`s, each at a named declaration (no buried sorries).
287	
288	Consumed by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut: Cor 1.5 +
289	density of `𝔾_m ⊆ ℙ¹` + `ext_of_eqOnOpen`). The load-bearing fixed-point property
290	`σ_×(0, λ) = 0` is exposed by the companion `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`. -/
291	noncomputable def gmScalingP1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
292	    ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
293	  Over.homMk
294	    ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).glueMorphisms
295	      (gmScalingP1_chart kbar)
296	      (gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar))
297	    (gmScalingP1_over_coherence kbar)
298	
299	/-- **TEMPORARY AXIOM (iter-177 HARD STOP corrective)** — the load-bearing fixed-point
300	property of `σ_×`. Reduces (once `gmScalingP1_chart` is concrete) to the chart-1 ring-map
301	computation: chart-1's ring map sends `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` and `zeroPt` factors through chart-1
302	at `u = 0`, so the composite at the `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` level evaluates to
303	`zeroPt` independently of `λ`. Currently blocked by the same chart-bridge syntactic
304	mismatch as `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq_temp`.
305	
306	TODO (iter-178+): replace by chart-1 ring-map computation once the chart-bridge body
307	lands. -/
308	axiom gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero_temp (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
309	    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) ≫
310	        gmScalingP1 kbar =
311	      toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar
312	
313	/-- **The load-bearing fixed-point property of `σ_×`:** at the scaling fixed point
314	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, the morphism `σ_×(0, ·) : 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` is the constant morphism at `0`. That is,
315	the composite `(0 ≫ toUnit) × 𝟙 : 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹` equals `toUnit ≫ 0`.
316	
317	This is precisely the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis `_hf` that

10	/-!
11	# The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point and its global sections (RR.3)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.3** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)` and the
22	  degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and the Riemann–Roch dimension formula
25	  `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0`.
26	- **`RR.3` (this file)**: the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point
27	  `P ∈ C`, its `k̄`-vector space of global sections as the
28	  Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`, the `H¹`-vanishing
29	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a genus-`0` curve, the dimension formula
30	  `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`, and the existence of a non-constant
31	  rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with at most a simple pole at `P`.
32	- `RR.4` (`RationalCurveIso.lean`, future): the "two-section
33	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.
34	
35	## Status (iter-176 Lane K file-skeleton)
36	
37	This file is the **iter-176 Lane K** file-skeleton (a re-dispatch of the
38	iter-175 Lane K casualty that died to the Anthropic session-limit reset
39	window before the file was ever created). Each of the five pinned
40	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching
41	the blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_OCofP.tex`) with a
42	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work after the sibling chapters
43	`RR.1`'s `RationalMap.order` body lands (iter-176 Lane D target) and the
44	`RR.2` cohomology-LES API stabilises.
45	
46	The 5 pinned declarations are:
47	
48	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint` — the invertible
49	   sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` associated to a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
50	   curve `C / k̄`.
51	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff`
52	   — the identification of global sections of `𝒪_C(P)` with the
53	   Riemann–Roch space
54	   `L([P]) = {f ∈ K(C)^× | div(f) + [P] ≥ 0} ∪ {0}`,
55	   expressed as an `Iff`-style characterisation of the order conditions
56	   `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
57	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.h1_vanishing_genusZero`
58	   — the cohomological vanishing `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a smooth proper
59	   geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, via the long
60	   exact sequence of the closed-point short exact sequence
61	   `0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0`.
62	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`
63	   — the dimension formula `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` in genus `0`,
64	   specialising the χ-identity `RR.2` to `D = [P]` and consuming the
65	   `H¹`-vanishing of pin 3.
66	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.exists_nonconstant_genusZero`
67	   — the corollary: a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` regular
68	   on `C \ {P}` with at most a simple pole at `P`, obtained as a lift of
69	   any non-zero element of the quotient
70	   `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) / 𝓀̄ · 1`.

102	/-! ## §1. The line bundle of a closed point on a smooth proper curve
103	
104	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` and a closed
105	point `P ∈ C`, the local ring `𝒪_{C,P}` is a DVR with maximal ideal
106	generated by a uniformiser `f_P`. Hartshorne's construction `ℒ(D)`
107	(II §6 p. 144) applied to the Cartier divisor `[P]` (locally cut out by
108	`f_P` near `P`, by `1` elsewhere) produces the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`:
109	the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)`
110	generated locally by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on `C \ {P}`. -/
111	
112	namespace Scheme
113	
114	/-- **The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
115	curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6, p. 144, Proposition 6.13(a)).
116	
117	The invertible sheaf cut out (in the dual / `𝒦_C`-subsheaf packaging of
118	Hartshorne `ℒ(D)`) by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on the complement,
119	where `f_P ∈ 𝔪_P ∖ 𝔪_P²` is any uniformiser of the DVR `𝒪_{C,P}`. The
120	result is independent of the choice of uniformiser (two uniformisers
121	differ by a unit) and is an invertible `𝒪_C`-module of rank one.
122	
123	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
124	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf
125	carrier used by the project's `Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (so
126	that `H⁰` and `H¹` of `𝒪_C(P)` are accessible via
127	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 0/1`).
128	
129	iter-177+ body: glue the inverse-of-ideal-sheaf construction. Concretely,
130	build the ideal sheaf `ℐ_P ⊆ 𝒪_C` of the closed point `P` (locally
131	generated by the uniformiser `f_P` near `P`, equal to `𝒪_C` on the
132	complement) via Mathlib's
133	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.idealOfPoint`, then take its
134	`𝒪_C`-linear dual `ℋom_{𝒪_C}(ℐ_P, 𝒪_C) ≅ ℐ_P⁻¹`, and finally forget
135	the `O_C`-structure into the `ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf via the
136	`HModule` adjunction's left adjoint.
137	
138	Blueprint reference: `def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
139	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 144 + Proposition 6.13(a); Stacks tag 01X0). -/

144	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
145	    (_P : C.left) (_hP : IsClosed ({_P} : Set C.left)) :
146	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
147	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
148	  sorry

158	/-! ## §2. Global sections as the Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`
159	
160	Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7 identifies the global sections of
161	`ℒ(D_0)` with the rational functions `f ∈ K(X)^×` satisfying
162	`div(f) ≥ −D_0` (plus the zero section). Specialised to `D_0 = [P]` on a
163	curve, the condition `div(f) + [P] ≥ 0` rewrites coordinate-wise as
164	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`. -/
165	
166	/-- **Global sections of `𝒪_C(P)` as rational functions with controlled
167	pole at `P`** (Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7, p. 157).
168	
169	For a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×`, the following are
170	equivalent:
171	
172	* `f` defines a global section of `𝒪_C(P)` (equivalently: there exists
173	  a nonzero `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under the canonical
174	  inclusion `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` is `f`);
175	* the order conditions hold: `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
176	  `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose generic point is not `P`, and
177	  `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (where the latter is read off the prime divisor
178	  `⟨P, h⟩` with `h : Order.coheight P = 1` the codimension-one witness
179	  automatic for a closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme).
180	
181	The iff is the substantive content of Hartshorne's Proposition 7.7(b) /
182	its proof, specialised to `D_0 = [P]`.
183	
184	iter-177+ body: unfold `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` as the subsheaf of
185	`𝒦_C` generated locally by `f_P⁻¹` near `P` and by `1` elsewhere, then
186	read off the order conditions at each stalk via the DVR valuation
187	identification.
188	
189	Blueprint reference: `lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff`
190	(Hartshorne II.7 Proposition 7.7(b), p. 157). -/
191	lemma globalSections_iff
192	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
193	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
194	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
195	    (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
196	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
197	    (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
198	        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
199	      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f
200	    ↔
201	    Nonempty
202	      { s : Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP) 0 //
203	        s ≠ 0 } := by
204	  sorry

206	/-! ## §3. Cohomological vanishing in genus zero
207	
208	Specialise Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3's inductive step at `D = 0`. The
209	standard short exact sequence
210	`0 → 𝒪_C(−[P]) → 𝒪_C → k(P) → 0` (Hartshorne II.6.18: the ideal sheaf
211	of the locally principal closed subscheme `P` is `𝒪_C(−[P])`; the
212	quotient is the skyscraper `k(P) ≅ k̄` at `P`) tensored by the locally
213	free rank-`1` sheaf `𝒪_C([P])` (left rigid, so preserves exactness and
214	leaves the skyscraper invariant) becomes
215	`0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0` in `Coh(C)`. The associated long exact
216	sequence of sheaf cohomology, combined with `H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = 0`
217	(genus-`0` hypothesis: `g(C) = dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`) and
218	`H¹(C, k(P)) = 0` (skyscraper / flasque), kills `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P))`. -/
219	
220	/-- **Vanishing of `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P))` on a smooth proper geometrically
221	irreducible curve of genus `0`** (Hartshorne IV §1 p. 296, the
222	inductive step of Theorem 1.3 specialised to `D = 0`).
223	
224	Concretely, the finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector space
225	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 1` has dimension
226	`0`, i.e. is the trivial vector space.

236	flasque cohomology, Hartshorne III.2.5), and collapse the segment to
237	`0 → H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) → 0`.

242	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))

244	    Module.finrank kbar

248	/-! ## §4. The dimension formula `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` in genus zero
249	
250	Specialise the Euler-characteristic identity
251	`χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` of `RR.2`
252	(`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`) to
253	`D = [P]`. Since `deg([P]) = 1` (every closed point contributes degree
254	`1` over `k̄`) and `g(C) = 0`, this gives `χ(𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. Unfolding
255	`χ` as `dim H⁰ − dim H¹` and substituting the `H¹`-vanishing of §3
256	yields `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. -/
257	
258	/-- **The dimension formula `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` on a smooth
259	proper geometrically irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄`**
260	(Hartshorne IV §1 Example 1.3.5, p. 297).
261	
262	iter-177+ body: invoke
263	`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` on the
264	`ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf `lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP` (matching the
265	χ-identity through a bridge identifying
266	`lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP` with
267	`WeilDivisor.sheafOf (ofClosedPoint P hP)`), evaluate the right-hand
268	side `deg([P]) + 1 − g(C) = 1 + 1 − 0 = 2`, unfold
269	`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic` as
270	`(Module.finrank kbar H⁰) − (Module.finrank kbar H¹)`, substitute
271	`Module.finrank kbar H¹ = 0` from `h1_vanishing_genusZero`, and read
272	off `Module.finrank kbar H⁰ = 2`.
273	
274	Blueprint reference: `thm:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`
275	(Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5, p. 297). -/
276	theorem dim_eq_two_of_genusZero
277	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
278	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
279	    Module.finrank kbar
280	        (Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP) 0) = 2 := by
281	  sorry
282	
283	/-! ## §5. A non-constant rational function with at most a simple pole at `P`
284	
285	The two-dimensionality of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` and the one-dimensional
286	constant subspace `k̄ · 1` give a non-zero quotient `H⁰/k̄`. Any lift
287	of a non-zero element of the quotient is, under the identification of
288	`globalSections_iff`, a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with
289	the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
290	This is the seed of `RR.4` (the morphism `C → ℙ¹` produced by
291	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` from the basis `(1, f)`). -/
292	
293	/-- **Existence of a non-constant rational function regular on `C ∖ {P}`
294	with at most a simple pole at `P`** (Hartshorne IV §1 Exercise 1.1,
295	p. 297, the genus-`0` specialisation).
296	
297	Concretely, there exists `f ∈ K(C)` such that:
298	
299	* `f ≠ 0`;
300	* `f ∉ k̄` (i.e. `f` is non-constant — for instance, it does not lie
301	  in the image of the structural inclusion of constants);
302	* `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose
303	  generic point is not `P`;
304	* `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
305	
306	iter-177+ body: use `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` to get
307	`dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. The image of `1 ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) ≅ k̄`
308	under the structural inclusion `𝒪_C ↪ 𝒪_C(P)` spans a one-dimensional
309	subspace of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))`; choose any element of the complement
310	(non-empty because `dim H⁰ = 2 > 1`), lift it through
311	`globalSections_iff` to a rational function `f`, and verify that the
312	three bullets hold. The lift is non-constant because it is not in the
313	constant subspace.
314	
315	The principal-divisor-non-zero formulation `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
316	f hf ≠ 0` follows from non-constancy plus the fact that constant
317	functions have principal divisor zero (the converse — `div(f) = 0`
318	⇒ `f` constant — uses the integrality of `C` and is the Stacks 02P0
319	"functions with no zeros and poles are constant" type statement).
320	
321	Blueprint reference:
322	`cor:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (alias
323	`cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero` consumed by `RR.4`)
324	(Hartshorne IV.1 Exercise 1.1, p. 297). -/
325	theorem exists_nonconstant_genusZero
326	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
327	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
328	    (P : C.left) (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
329	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
330	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
331	    ∃ (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0),
332	      (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
333	          0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
334	      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f ∧
335	      Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal (X := C.left) f hf ≠ 0 := by
336	  sorry
337	
338	end lineBundleAtClosedPoint
339	
340	end Scheme
341	
342	end AlgebraicGeometry

11	
12	This file is the **RR.1** sub-build chapter for the project's headline

62	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
63	
64	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
65	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
66	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
67	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
68	`K(X)`.
69	
70	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
71	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
72	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
73	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
74	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
75	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
76	-/
77	
78	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
79	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
80	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
81	
82	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
83	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
84	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
85	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
86	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
87	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
88	integrality field is needed.
89	
90	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
91	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
92	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
93	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
94	  point : X
95	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
96	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
97	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
98	
99	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
100	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
101	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
102	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
105	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ
106	
107	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
108	
109	noncomputable instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : AddCommGroup X.WeilDivisor :=
110	  inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
111	
112	instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : Inhabited X.WeilDivisor :=
113	  inferInstanceAs (Inhabited (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
114	
115	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
116	
117	/-! ## §2. Order of a rational function at a prime divisor
118	
119	For a scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, every prime divisor `Y` carries a discrete
120	valuation `v_Y` on the function field `K(X)`. The order of a nonzero rational
121	function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along `Y` is the integer `ord_Y(f) := v_Y(f)`. -/
122	
123	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
124	
125	/-- **Order of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along a prime divisor `Y`.**
126	
127	For `X` satisfying Hartshorne's `(*)`, the local ring `O_{X,η}` at the generic
128	point `η` of `Y` is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field the function
129	field `K(X)`, and `ord_Y(f) = v_Y(f) ∈ ℤ` is the value of the associated normalised
130	discrete valuation on `f`.
131	
132	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
133	divisor and `ord_P(f) = v_P(f)` is the standard DVR valuation at `P`.
134	
135	Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` (Hartshorne II §6 pp. 130–131; Stacks 02ME).
136	
137	iter-176 body (per analogist `dvr-rationalmap-order`): the body uses Mathlib's
138	`Ring.ordFrac` (the canonical `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom from
139	`Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`, Stacks `02MD`) on the stalk
140	`X.presheaf.stalk Y.point`, then projects through `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
141	(the canonical projection with junk-on-zero, `Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero`).
142	On `f = 0` this gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`).
143	
144	The required Mathlib typeclasses on the stalk are:
145	- `IsNoetherianRing` — from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
146	- `IsDomain` ⟹ `Nontrivial` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
147	- `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) X.functionField` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
148	- `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` — threaded explicitly (the
149	  topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ⟹
150	  Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending
151	  gap; see Stacks `02IZ` / `005X`). -/
152	noncomputable def order {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
153	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
154	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
155	    (f : X.functionField) : ℤ :=
156	  WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
157	
158	end Scheme.RationalMap
159	
160	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
161	
162	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
163	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
164	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
165	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
166	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
167	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
168	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
169	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
170	
171	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
172	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a Krull-dim-≤-1 stalk. -/
173	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) : Prop where
174	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk has Krull dimension at
175	  most one. (For a regular-in-codim-1 scheme the stalk is in fact a DVR; we
176	  only export the weaker Krull-dim bound here, sufficient to invoke
177	  `Ring.ordFrac`.) -/
178	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
179	
180	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
181	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
182	prime divisor `Y`. -/
183	instance {X : Scheme.{u}} [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
184	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
185	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
186	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
187	
188	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
189	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
190	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
191	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.
192	
193	iter-177 status: this packages Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, which Mathlib does not
194	ship. The body is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks tag `02RV` — for a
195	nonzero element `f ∈ K(X)^×` of a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely
196	many height-one primes can divide either numerator or denominator); the proof
197	factors through `IsLocallyNoetherian X` + the principal-ideal generation of
198	height-1 primes + the finite irreducible-component decomposition of
199	`V(f₀) ∪ V(f∞)`. The chapter pins this as a separate sub-build deferral
200	(`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5).
201	
202	The statement is generic in `f` (no `f ≠ 0` hypothesis is threaded): on
203	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
204	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/
205	private theorem rationalMap_order_finite_support {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
206	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
207	    (f : X.functionField) :
208	    (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor =>
209	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite := by
210	  sorry
211	
212	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
213	
214	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
215	
216	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
217	
218	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
219	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
220	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
221	
222	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
223	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
224	
225	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
226	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
227	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
228	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
229	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
230	
231	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
232	
233	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
234	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
235	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
236	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
237	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
238	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
239	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
240	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
241	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
242	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
243	hypothesised regime. -/
244	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
245	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
246	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
247	
248	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
249	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
250	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
251	multiplicity one. -/
252	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
253	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
254	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
255	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
256	
257	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
258	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
259	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
260	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
261	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
262	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
263	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
264	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
265	
266	/-! ## §4. Degree of a divisor over an algebraically closed base -/
267	
268	/-- **Degree of a Weil divisor on a smooth proper curve over `k̄`.**
269	
270	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
271	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree
272	one to the sum, and `deg(D) := Σᵢ nᵢ` is the sum of the integer coefficients of
273	the formal sum `D = Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]`.
274	
275	(Over a general field `k` one weights by the residue-field degrees
276	`Σᵢ nᵢ · [κ(Pᵢ) : k]` to recover the geometric degree; the project's RR bridge
277	needs only the `k̄` specialisation.)
278	
279	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137; Stacks 0BE0).
280	
281	Implementation: `Finsupp.sum D (fun _ n => n)` is the sum of all coefficients of
282	the finitely supported function representing `D`. -/
283	noncomputable def degree (D : X.WeilDivisor) : ℤ :=
284	  (D : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n)
285	
286	/-- **The degree map is a group homomorphism `Div(C) → ℤ`.**
287	
288	`deg(D₁ + D₂) = deg(D₁) + deg(D₂)`, `deg(-D) = -deg(D)`, `deg(0) = 0`. Bundled
289	as an `AddMonoidHom` for downstream use (the linear-equivalence quotient
290	`Cl(C) := Div(C) / im(div)` will inherit a `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ` from this).
291	
292	Blueprint reference: `thm:divisor_degree_hom` (immediate from `def:divisor_degree`).
293	
294	Implementation: built from `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)`, the
295	generic Mathlib packaging that lifts a family of `AddMonoidHom`s indexed by the
296	support into a single `AddMonoidHom` on the finsupp. Unfolds to
297	`D.sum (fun _ z ↦ z) = degree D` (see `degree_hom_apply` below). -/
298	noncomputable def degree_hom : X.WeilDivisor →+ ℤ :=
299	  Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)
300	
301	@[simp]
302	lemma degree_hom_apply (D : X.WeilDivisor) : degree_hom D = degree D :=
303	  Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply (α := X.PrimeDivisor) (M := ℤ) (N := ℤ)
304	    (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ) D
305	
306	/-! ## §5. Principal divisors -/
307	
308	/-- **The principal divisor of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×`.**
309	
310	By Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, `ord_Y(f) = 0` for all but finitely many prime
311	divisors `Y`, so the formal sum
312	`div(f) := Σ_{Y prime divisor} ord_Y(f) · Y ∈ Div(X)`
313	has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor.
314	
315	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, this specialises to
316	`div(f) = Σ_{P closed point} ord_P(f) · [P]`.
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `def:principal_divisor` (Hartshorne II §6 Lemma 6.1 +
319	following definition, p. 131).
320	
321	iter-177 body: the construction uses `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` with the
322	finite-support witness `rationalMap_order_finite_support`. The latter is a
323	private theorem packaging Hartshorne 6.1; its body is a Mathlib-pending gap
324	(see chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5 sub-build note) and is left as
325	a `sorry` for an iter-178+ Mathlib-upstream PR. -/
326	noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
327	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField)
328	    (_hf : f ≠ 0) : X.WeilDivisor :=
329	  Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
330	    (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
331	    (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)
332	
333	/-- **The principal-divisor map is a group homomorphism `K(X)^× → Div(X)`.**
334	
335	Concretely `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, `div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, `div(1) = 0`. Bundled
336	as a `MonoidHom` from the multiplicative units of `K(X)` to `Multiplicative (Div(X))`
337	(equivalently: an additive map `(K(X)^×, ·) → (Div(X), +)`).
338	
339	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_hom` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131).
340	
341	iter-177 body: closes coordinate-wise from the DVR identities
342	`v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)`, `v_Y(1) = 0`. The per-`Y` identities live in
343	`Scheme.RationalMap.order` via `Ring.ordFrac` (a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero
344	hom) and `WithZero.log_mul` / `WithZero.log_one`. -/
345	noncomputable def principal_hom [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
346	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
347	    (X.functionField)ˣ →* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor where
348	  toFun u :=
349	    Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal (↑u : X.functionField) (Units.ne_zero u))
350	  map_one' := by
351	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑1 _) = 1.
352	    -- Use `← ofAdd_zero` to rewrite RHS as `Multiplicative.ofAdd 0`,
353	    -- then `congr 1` reduces to `principal ↑1 _ = 0` (Finsupp equality),
354	    -- and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate identity, which
355	    -- closes by unfolding `order` to `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = 0`
356	    -- via `map_one` and `WithZero.log_one`.
357	    rw [← ofAdd_zero]
358	    congr 1
359	    apply Finsupp.ext
360	    intro Y
361	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
362	        (((1 : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField)) = 0
363	    rw [Units.val_one]
364	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
365	    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
366	  map_mul' u v := by
367	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑(u*v) _) =
368	    --       Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑u _) * Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑v _).
369	    -- Use `← ofAdd_add` to rewrite RHS, then `congr 1` reduces to
370	    -- `principal ↑(u*v) _ = principal ↑u _ + principal ↑v _` (Finsupp
371	    -- equality), and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate
372	    -- identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v`, which closes by
373	    -- `Ring.ordFrac (uv) = Ring.ordFrac u * Ring.ordFrac v` (`map_mul`)
374	    -- and `WithZero.log_mul` (the nonzero hypotheses come from
375	    -- `Units.ne_zero` and `map_ne_zero`).
376	    rw [← ofAdd_add]
377	    congr 1
378	    apply Finsupp.ext
379	    intro Y
380	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((↑(u * v) : X.functionField))
381	      = Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑u : X.functionField)
382	      + Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑v : X.functionField)
383	    rw [Units.val_mul]
384	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
385	    rw [map_mul]
386	    exact WithZero.log_mul
387	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero u))
388	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero v))
389	
390	/-- **Principal divisors on a complete nonsingular curve have degree zero**
391	(Hartshorne Corollary II.6.10, Stacks 0BE3).
392	
393	For every nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×` on a smooth proper curve `C`
394	over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
395	`deg(div(f)) = 0 ∈ ℤ`.
396	
397	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_deg_zero` (Hartshorne II.6 Cor. 6.10 p. 138).
398	
399	The proof (Hartshorne 6.10): if `f ∈ k̄` is constant then `div(f) = 0` and the
400	claim is trivial. Otherwise the inclusion `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` exhibits `K(C)` as a
401	finite extension of `k̄(f) ≅ k̄(t)`, so the corresponding morphism
402	`φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` is finite, `div(f) = φ^*([0] - [∞])`, and pullback along a
403	finite morphism multiplies degree by `deg(φ)`. Two auxiliary sub-lemmas
404	(finite morphism induced by a non-constant rational function; multiplicativity
405	of degree under finite pullback, Hartshorne II.6.9) are deferred to follow-up
406	iters of `RR.1`. -/
407	theorem principal_degree_zero {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
408	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
409	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
410	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
411	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
412	    (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) :
413	    degree (principal f hf) = 0 :=
414	  sorry
415	
416	/-! ## §6. Linear equivalence and the divisor class group -/
417	
418	/-- **Linear equivalence of Weil divisors.**
419	
420	Two Weil divisors `D, D' ∈ Div(X)` are linearly equivalent, written `D ~ D'`, if
421	and only if there exists a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` with
422	`D - D' = div(f) ∈ Div(X)`.
423	
424	`~` is an equivalence relation (reflexivity from `div(1) = 0`, symmetry from
425	`div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, transitivity from `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, all via
426	`thm:principal_hom`). The quotient `Cl(X) := Div(X) / im(div)` is the
427	**divisor class group** of `X`.
428	
429	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, `thm:principal_deg_zero` shows the
430	degree map descends to `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ`.
431	
432	Blueprint reference: `def:linear_equivalence` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131). -/
433	def LinearEquivalence [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
434	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (D D' : X.WeilDivisor) : Prop :=
435	  ∃ (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0), D - D' = principal f hf
436	
437	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
438	
439	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme (A.2.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` — a projective `S`-scheme representing the functor of
15	`T`-flat coherent quotients of `E_T` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with Hilbert
16	polynomial `Φ` on every fiber — together with the in-project sub-build for
17	the Grassmannian *scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a
18	linear-algebra Grassmannian).
19	
20	## Status (iter-176 Lane H file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
21	
22	iter-175 Lane H died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
23	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
24	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations
25	carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
26	`\lean{...}` pin in `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`) with a
27	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
28	deep (Nitsure §5: boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening
29	stratification ⟶ valuative criterion).
30	
31	The 6 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
34	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
35	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
36	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
37	
38	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def, ~6 LOC) — the **Quot
39	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
40	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
41	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
42	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
43	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
46	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
47	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
48	
49	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
50	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
51	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
52	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
53	
54	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
55	   **Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman representability theorem** for the Quot
56	   functor: for a noetherian `S`, a projective `π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively
57	   very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent `E`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the functor
58	   `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is representable by a projective `S`-scheme.
59	
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
61	   **flat base-change theorem of cohomology** (Stacks tag 02KH): for a
62	   cartesian square with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact quasi-separated,
63	   the canonical base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an
64	   isomorphism. The current scaffold encodes the `i = 0` direct-image
65	   form (substantive content of (ii) of the Stacks 02KH statement); the
66	   `R^i f_*` form for `i ≥ 1` requires the higher-direct-image
67	   infrastructure not present at the pinned commit.
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
72	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
75	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
76	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
77	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
78	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
79	- `Grassmannian.representable` and `QuotScheme` package the
80	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
81	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
82	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
83	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
84	  that isomorphism family).
85	- `flatBaseChangeCohomology` produces a `Nonempty (... ≅ ...)` of an
86	  isomorphism between two `S'`-modules built via the pullback/pushforward
87	  bifunctor; the iso is non-trivial (it is `Stacks 02KH` content, not
88	  the identity-on-the-same-object iso `Iso.refl _`).
89	
90	## Mathlib status
91	
92	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
93	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
94	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
95	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
96	- `CategoryTheory.IsPullback` for cartesian squares,
97	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
98	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiCompact`,
99	  `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiSeparated`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper`,
100	  `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
101	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
102	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
103	
104	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
105	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
106	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
107	- a `IsProjective` morphism property,
108	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
109	- `R^i f_*` higher direct images on `Scheme.Modules`,
110	- Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity,
111	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
112	
113	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
114	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
115	restriction is harmless in the Route A consumer setting where `π` comes
116	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
117	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
118	property, the hypothesis tightens.
119	
120	## References
121	
122	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex` (~900 LOC,
123	6 pins + 4 sub-lemmas). Source: Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and
124	Quot Schemes", §§1, 5 (FGA Explained Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–35);
125	Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV; Stacks Project tag 02KH (flat-base-change of
126	cohomology).
