94	universe u
95	
96	open CategoryTheory Limits
97	
98	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
99	
100	/-! ## §1. The Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve -/
101	
102	/-- **Euler characteristic of a `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf on a smooth proper
103	curve `C / k̄`.**
104	
105	On a curve (one-dimensional scheme), Grothendieck vanishing
106	(Hartshorne III.2.7) gives `H^i(C, 𝓕) = 0` for `i ≥ 2`, so the classical
107	alternating sum
108	`χ(𝓕) = Σ_{i ≥ 0} (-1)^i dim_{k̄} H^i(C, 𝓕)` collapses to the two-term
109	expression
110	`χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)`.
111	
112	This is the definition we ship. Coherence of `𝓕` on the proper `k̄`-scheme
113	`C` guarantees that both `H⁰` and `H¹` are finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector
114	spaces (Serre's coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem, the same
115	finiteness backing `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`), so the two `Module.finrank`s
116	are honest natural numbers and the difference is a well-defined integer.
117	
118	The `Module k̄`-valued cohomology pipeline is the project's
119	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009), the same wrapper used by
120	`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`.
121	
122	Blueprint reference: `def:eulerChar_curve`
123	(Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295, displayed inside the proof of Theorem 1.3). -/
124	noncomputable def Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
125	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
126	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
127	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
128	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
129	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
130	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) : ℤ :=
131	  (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 0) : ℤ)
132	    - (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 1) : ℤ)
133	
134	/-! ## §2. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (placeholder)
135	
136	The honest construction of `𝒪_C(D)` lives in the sibling chapter `RR.3`
137	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`), where the locally-principal ideal sheaves of
138	closed points are glued into an invertible `𝒪_C`-module. To keep the
139	iter-174 skeleton's pin signatures substantive, we expose `𝒪_C(D)` as a
140	typed-`sorry` `ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf-on-`C`. The iter-175+ closure of
141	`RR.3` will replace the body with the construction; downstream pins (the
142	`ℓ`-invariant, the χ-identity, the genus-`0` Riemann–Roch formula) consume
143	this placeholder only through its `H⁰` and `H¹`, so their signatures
144	encode arithmetic identities on finiteness-of-cohomology outputs and are
145	not vacuous. -/
146	
147	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
148	
149	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
150	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
151	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
152	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
153	
154	/-- **The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper
155	curve `C / k̄`** (placeholder for `RR.3`).
156	
157	For a Weil divisor `D = Σᵢ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` on a smooth proper geometrically
158	irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the associated invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` is the
159	locally-free `𝒪_C`-module of rank one cut out (locally near each `Pᵢ`) by
160	the `(−nᵢ)`-th power of the local uniformiser at `Pᵢ`. Hartshorne II §6
161	Proposition 6.13 packages this construction; the project-side `RR.3`
162	chapter (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, to be added) will produce the carrier as
163	a `Sheaf` of `ModuleCat k̄`-modules on `C.left`.
164	
165	In the iter-174 skeleton this is a typed-`sorry`. The body in `RR.3` will
166	be the invertible-sheaf glue of the locally principal ideals of the
167	support of `D`. -/
168	noncomputable def sheafOf (_D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
169	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
170	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
171	  sorry
172	
173	/-! ## §3. The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor -/
174	
175	/-- **The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve
176	`C / k̄`.**
177	
178	By definition,
179	`ℓ(D) := dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D)) ∈ ℕ`,
180	the `k̄`-dimension of the space of global sections of the invertible sheaf
181	`𝒪_C(D)` associated to `D`. Finiteness of `ℓ(D)` is a consequence of the
182	coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem on a proper `k̄`-scheme
183	(Hartshorne II.5.19 / III.5.2), the same input that backs the finiteness
184	of `genus C`.
185	
186	The classical *complete linear system* `|D|` has `k̄`-projective dimension
187	`ℓ(D) − 1`; the chapter never uses this projective interpretation —
188	`ℓ(D)` is the only quantity consumed by the Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:l_invariant` (Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295,
191	"We denote `dim_k H⁰(X, 𝒪(D))` by `l(D)`"). -/
192	noncomputable def l (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) : ℕ :=
193	  Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf (C := C) D) 0)
194	
195	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
196	
197	/-! ## §4. The χ-identity: `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`
198	
199	The bridge from the structural Euler characteristic to the arithmetic
200	degree of a divisor. Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3 reduces Riemann–Roch to
201	this identity; the proof is the inductive `D ↔ D + [P]` step (additivity
202	of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence, with base case `D = 0`
203	giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` from `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` and the definition of
204	the genus). The closure of the body is iter-175+ work after the
205	`Euler-characteristic additivity on a short exact sequence`-style
206	project-side helper is supplied and the `RR.3` sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` has a
207	body. -/
208	
209	/-- **Euler-characteristic identity for `𝒪_C(D)` on a smooth proper curve
210	of genus `g`.**
211	
212	For every Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)`,
213	`χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g(C)`.
214	
215	The proof is Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3's reduction: induction on the
216	free-abelian-group structure of `Div(C)` on closed points, base case `D =
217	0` giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` (the `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` is the Hartshorne
218	I.3.4 input via the project's `H⁰`-bridge), and inductive step via
219	additivity of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence
220	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`.
221	
222	Blueprint reference: `thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus`
223	(Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3, p. 295). -/
224	theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus
225	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
226	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
227	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
228	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
229	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
230	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D)
231	      = (Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D) + 1 - (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ℤ) :=
232	  sorry
233	
234	/-! ## §5. The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero -/
235	
236	/-- **Riemann–Roch in genus zero (Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5).**
237	
238	Let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over the
239	algebraically closed field `k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, and let `D ∈ Div(C)` with
240	`deg D ≥ 0`. Then
241	`ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1`.
242	
243	The proof specialises the χ-identity
244	`eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` to `g = 0`,
245	unfolds `χ` via `def:eulerChar_curve`, and absorbs the `H¹`-vanishing
246	hypothesis (named premise `_hH1`: `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0`, which is the
247	`H¹`-vanishing of a non-negative-degree invertible sheaf on a
248	genus-`0` curve, to be discharged by `RR.3` once `𝒪_C(D)` has a body and
249	the cohomology of `𝒪_{ℙ¹}(d)` is computed).
250	
251	Blueprint reference: `thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero` (Hartshorne IV.1
252	Example 1.3.5, p. 297). -/
253	theorem Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero
254	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
255	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
256	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
257	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
258	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0)
259	    (_hdeg : (0 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D)
260	    (_hH1 : Module.finrank kbar
261	      (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) 1) = 0) :
262	    (Scheme.WeilDivisor.l (C := C) D : ℤ)
263	      = Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D + 1 :=
264	  sorry
265	
266	end AlgebraicGeometry
267

8	/-!
9	# Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (A.4.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.4.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula and the corollary "regular local ring
14	⟹ Cohen–Macaulay" that the sibling sub-build **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
15	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective surface)
16	consumes.
17	
18	Per STRATEGY.md L30 this row is gated downstream on A.4.a but is independently
19	startable on the Mathlib side: the algebra here is decoupled from the Albanese
20	geometry and lives entirely under `RingTheory.*`. Mathlib at the project's
21	pinned commit (`b80f227`) exposes `IsRegularLocalRing` and the categorical
22	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on an abelian category (specialised to
23	`ModuleCat R`), as well as the regular-sequence definition
24	`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`. The depth function `Module.depth`, the
25	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula, and the Cohen–Macaulay predicate are *not* in
26	Mathlib at the pinned commit — they are the new content scaffolded here.
27	
28	## Status (iter-175 Lane F file-skeleton)
29	
30	This file is the **iter-175 Lane F** file-skeleton: each of the seven
31	blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive type
32	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
33	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
34	The bodies are iter-176+ work; the chapter is independent of the rest of
35	Route A's geometric infrastructure, making it a good parallel-work candidate.
36	
37	The 7 pinned declarations are:
38	
39	1. `RingTheory.Module.depth` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the `I`-depth of a
40	   finite `R`-module as the supremum of lengths of `M`-regular sequences in
41	   `I`, valued in `ℕ∞`.
42	2. `RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — for
43	   `(R, 𝔪)` Noetherian local and `M ≠ 0` finite, `depth(M)` equals the
44	   smallest `i` with `Ext^i_R(κ, M) ≠ 0`. Encoded via the depth-bound iff
45	   `Ext` vanishes below.
46	3. `Module.projectiveDimension` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) — an
47	   `R`-module-side wrapper for the categorical
48	   `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)`. The categorical
49	   version exists in Mathlib `b80f227`; this is the re-export pinned by the
50	   blueprint.
51	4. `RingTheory.depth_of_short_exact` (theorem, ~12 LOC) — the
52	   depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence inequalities (Stacks 00LE).
53	5. `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the formula
54	   `pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)` for a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite
55	   projective dimension over a Noetherian local ring.
56	6. `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay` (class, ~3 LOC) — `IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing R`
57	   encoded as `depth(R) = krullDim R`.
58	7. `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular` (theorem, ~6 LOC) — a regular local
59	   ring is Cohen–Macaulay (the consumer-facing input for A.4.a).
60	
61	## Note on type expressivity
62	
63	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
64	declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
65	
66	- `depth` returns `ℕ∞` and is defined by the regular-sequence supremum.
67	- `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` is encoded as the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`
68	  vanishing characterisation (the smallest non-vanishing `Ext` index = depth).
69	- `projectiveDimension` re-exports the categorical
70	  `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on `ModuleCat.of R M`.
71	- `depth_of_short_exact` packages the three Stacks 00LE inequalities into a
72	  conjunction.
73	- `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` is the numeric equation
74	  `pd + depth = depth(R)`.
75	- `CohenMacaulay` is the equation `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`.
76	- `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` is an `IsRegularLocalRing → IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing`
77	  implication, the consumer-facing statement.
78	
79	Unfolding any declaration exposes the named substantive content (a regular-
80	sequence supremum, an `Ext`-vanishing characterisation, the categorical
81	projective dimension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` /
82	empty-content `proof_wanted` placeholders are used.

104	/-! ## §1. Depth of a finite module
105	
106	The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
107	`{0, 1, 2, …, ∞}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
108	(provided `IM ≠ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`). Mathlib `b80f227`
109	exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
110	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
111	numeric depth function — that is the gap this declaration fills.
112	
113	Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/
114	
115	/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `ℕ∞`) of
116	lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.
117	
118	When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
119	supremum is taken to be `⊤` by convention. When `(R, 𝔪)` is local one usually
120	calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.
121	
122	iter-176+: the body is the supremum
123	```
124	sSup { (n : ℕ∞) | ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧
125	                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
126	```
127	folded with the `IM = M` clause. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the body is a
128	typed `sorry`. The signature is non-tautological: it asserts a function
129	`(Ideal R) → (M : Type v) → ℕ∞` matching the Stacks 00LF definition. -/
130	noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
131	    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
132	  sorry
133	
134	end Module
135	
136	end RingTheory

138	/-! ## §2. Projective dimension
139	
140	Mathlib `b80f227` exposes the categorical
141	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension : C → WithBot ℕ∞` on an abelian category
142	with enough projectives (file `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Projective.Dimension`).
143	For `R`-modules this specialises to `ModuleCat.of R M`. The blueprint pins the
144	`Module.projectiveDimension` name as the re-export that downstream consumers
145	can use directly on an `R`-module without first packaging it in `ModuleCat`.
146	
147	Blueprint reference: `def:projective_dimension`. -/
148	
149	namespace Module
150	
151	/-- The **projective dimension** of an `R`-module `M`, defined as the
152	categorical projective dimension of `ModuleCat.of R M`.
153	
154	This is a re-export of `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` specialised to
155	the abelian category `ModuleCat R`. The categorical definition is the
156	infimum (in `WithBot ℕ∞`) of `n : ℕ` such that all `Ext^i(M, -)` vanish for
157	`i > n`, equivalently the smallest length of a projective resolution of `M`.
158	
159	Mathlib has the categorical `projectiveDimension` and the module-specific
160	`ModuleCat.projectiveDimension_eq_of_linearEquiv`; the blueprint pins the
161	wrapper name so downstream consumers can write
162	`Module.projectiveDimension R M` rather than threading `ModuleCat.of`.
163	
164	iter-176+: body is `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)`.
165	For the iter-175 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry` — the wrapper is
166	genuinely a one-liner once the universe / abbreviation choices are settled,
167	but we leave it as a typed `sorry` to keep the file-skeleton uniform. -/
168	noncomputable def projectiveDimension (R : Type u) [Ring R]
169	    (_M : Type u) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : WithBot ℕ∞ :=
170	  sorry
171	
172	end Module

174	namespace RingTheory

178	/-! ## §3. Depth via Ext characterisation
179	
180	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a
181	nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the depth of `M` equals the smallest index `i`
182	at which `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero (Stacks tag 00LP).
183	
184	This is the lemma A.4.a's downstream consumer ultimately reads off:
185	combined with the regular-sequence definition (`depth`), the Ext
186	characterisation provides both the *lower bound* (regular sequences exhibit
187	`Ext^i = 0` for `i < length(rs)`) and the *upper bound* (failure of any
188	extension lifts a non-zero element in `Ext^{depth}(κ, M)`).
189	
190	The signature pins the equivalence via the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`-vanishing-
191	below: `n ≤ depth(M) ↔ Ext^i(κ, M) = 0 for all i < n`. This is logically
192	equivalent to "depth(M) = smallest i with Ext^i ≠ 0" and is the form most
193	convenient for inductive proofs (Stacks 00LP proof: pick `x ∈ 𝔪`
194	non-zero-divisor, use long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`).
195	
196	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_via_ext` (Stacks tag 00LP). -/
197	
198	/-- **Depth via Ext characterisation.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
199	with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the
200	depth-bound `n ≤ depth(M)` is equivalent to the vanishing of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)`
201	for all `i < n`. Equivalently, `depth(M)` is the smallest `i` at which
202	`Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero.
203	
204	iter-176+: the body proceeds by induction on `n` via the long exact sequence
205	of `Ext^*(κ, -)` applied to `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0` for a non-zero-divisor
206	`x ∈ 𝔪`. The base case `n = 0` is `Hom(κ, M) ≠ 0 ↔ depth(M) = 0`, which is
207	the standard "the maximal ideal contains a zero-divisor on `M` iff
208	`𝔪 ∈ Ass(M)`" characterisation (Stacks 00LC). For the iter-175 file-skeleton
209	the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
210	theorem depth_eq_smallest_ext_index
211	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
212	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
213	    [Nontrivial M] (n : ℕ) :
214	    (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M ↔
215	      ∀ i : ℕ, i < n →
216	        ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
217	            (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
218	            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 := by
219	  sorry
220	
221	/-! ## §4. Depth on a short exact sequence
222	
223	For a short exact sequence `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` of nonzero finite modules
224	over a Noetherian local ring, the three modules' depths satisfy three
225	crosswise inequalities (Stacks tag 00LE), each a direct read-off of the
226	long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` sequence and the depth-via-Ext characterisation
227	of §3.
228	
229	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_short_exact_sequence` (Stacks tag 00LE). -/
230	
231	/-- **Depth on a short exact sequence.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
232	ring and let
233	```
234	0 → N' → N → N'' → 0
235	```
236	be a short exact sequence of nonzero finite `R`-modules (encoded by an
237	injection `f : N' →ₗ[R] N`, a surjection `g : N →ₗ[R] N''`, and
238	`Function.Exact f g`). Then
239	1. `depth(N) ≥ min(depth(N'), depth(N''))`,
240	2. `depth(N'') ≥ min(depth(N), depth(N') - 1)`,
241	3. `depth(N') ≥ min(depth(N), depth(N'') + 1)`.
242	
243	The three inequalities are read off from the long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)`
244	sequence applied to the short exact sequence and the depth-via-Ext
245	characterisation (§3 above).
246	
247	iter-176+: the body invokes `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on each module
248	and chases the long exact sequence. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the
249	body is a typed `sorry`. -/
250	theorem depth_of_short_exact
251	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
252	    {N' N N'' : Type u}
253	    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
254	    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
255	    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
256	    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
257	    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
258	    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
259	    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
260	        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
261	      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
262	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
263	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
264	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
265	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
266	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
267	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
268	  sorry
269	
270	end Module
271	
272	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
273	
274	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
275	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
276	```
277	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
278	```
279	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
280	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
281	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
282	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
283	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
284	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
285	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
286	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
287	
288	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
289	
290	/-- **The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
291	ring and let `M` be a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite projective
292	dimension. Then
293	```
294	  pd_R(M) + depth_R(M) = depth(R).
295	```
296	
297	The hypothesis "finite projective dimension" is encoded by an explicit
298	upper bound `n : ℕ` on the projective dimension (so the formula compares
299	finite numeric quantities cleanly without `WithBot ℕ∞`-arithmetic
300	subtleties).
301	
302	iter-176+: the body is the induction on `depth(M)` outlined in the
303	chapter — base case via the minimal-finite-free-resolution +
304	"what is exact" + iterated `Module.depth_of_short_exact`; inductive step
305	via the snake lemma on multiplication by a common non-zero-divisor and
306	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`. For the iter-175
307	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
308	theorem auslander_buchsbaum_formula
309	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
310	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
311	    [Nontrivial M]
312	    (n : ℕ)
313	    (_hpd : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M = (n : WithBot ℕ∞)) :
314	    (n : ℕ∞) + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
315	      = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R := by
316	  sorry
317	
318	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings
319	
320	A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth equals
321	its Krull dimension (Stacks tag 00N4). Mathlib `b80f227` has neither the
322	predicate nor the class — this file is the upstream gap-fill.
323	
324	Blueprint reference: `def:cohen_macaulay_local` (Stacks tag 00N4). -/
325	
326	/-- A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth
327	equals its Krull dimension: `depth(R) = dim R`.
328	
329	Encoded as a `Prop`-valued type class so downstream consumers can write
330	`[CohenMacaulay R]` and use Cohen–Macaulay as a hypothesis. Mathlib at the
331	pinned commit (`b80f227`) does not expose any Cohen–Macaulay predicate;
332	this is the upstream gap-fill.
333	
334	iter-176+: the predicate is `Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R =
335	ringKrullDim R`. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the carrier definition is a
336	typed `sorry` at the `Prop` level — substantively, the predicate is the
337	named equality, but we package it as a `class` so use sites are uniform. -/
338	class CohenMacaulay (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
339	    [IsNoetherianRing R] : Prop where
340	  /-- The Cohen–Macaulay equation: `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`. The numeric
341	  comparison is in `WithBot ℕ∞` after coercion of the `ℕ∞`-valued depth. -/
342	  depth_eq_krullDim :
343	    (Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R : WithBot ℕ∞) = ringKrullDim R
344	
345	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
346	
347	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
348	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
349	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
350	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
351	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
352	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
353	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
354	
355	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
356	
357	namespace CohenMacaulay
358	
359	/-- **Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay.** Every regular Noetherian
360	local ring is Cohen–Macaulay: a minimal generating set of `𝔪` is an
361	`R`-regular sequence of length `dim R`, so `depth(R) ≥ dim R`; combined
362	with the standard upper bound `depth(R) ≤ dim R` (Stacks 00LK) this gives
363	`depth(R) = dim R`.
364	
365	This is the consumer-facing input for **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
366	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective
367	surface): the local ring `O_{S,x}` of a regular projective surface at a
368	closed point is regular of Krull dimension `2`, hence Cohen–Macaulay,
369	hence has depth `2`, which is exactly the input the local-cohomology
370	vanishing `H^i_x(O_S) = 0` for `i < 2` needs (Stacks 0AVF; see Hartshorne
371	III.7).
372	
373	iter-176+: the body is either the direct regular-sequence argument
374	outlined in the chapter, or via Auslander–Buchsbaum applied to all finite
375	`R`-modules (any finite `R`-module has `pd_R(M) ≤ d`; specialising to
376	suitable `M` gives the equation). For the iter-175 file-skeleton the body
377	is a typed `sorry`. -/
378	instance of_regular (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
379	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] : CohenMacaulay R where
380	  depth_eq_krullDim := by
381	    sorry
382	
383	end CohenMacaulay
384	
385	end RingTheory

8	/-!
9	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
10	variety extends (A.4.c)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
14	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
15	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
16	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
17	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
18	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
19	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
20	
21	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
22	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
23	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
24	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
25	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
26	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
27	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
28	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
29	defined.
30	
31	## Status (iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton)
32	
33	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
34	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
35	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in
36	`chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50) with a `sorry`
37	body. The body is iter-176+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
38	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (A.4.a) and
39	`Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b) landing their substantive
40	statements.
41	
42	The 1 pinned declaration is:
43	
44	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
45	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
46	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and
47	   `f : X ⇢ A` a rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
48	   `g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
49	
50	## Note on type expressivity
51	
52	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
53	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
54	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
55	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
56	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
57	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
58	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
59	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`).
60	
61	## Abelian-variety conventions
62	
63	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
64	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
65	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
66	
67	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
68	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
69	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
70	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
71	
72	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
73	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
74	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
75	
76	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
77	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
78	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
79	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
80	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
81	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
82	
83	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
84	the call site.
85	
86	## References
87	
88	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`
89	(250 LOC, 1 pin). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.2,
90	p. 17 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`, PDF page 23).
91	-/

93	set_option autoImplicit false

95	universe u
96	
97	open CategoryTheory Limits
98	
99	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
100	
101	namespace Scheme
102	
103	namespace RationalMap
104	
105	/-! ## Milne Theorem 3.2 — rational maps to an abelian variety extend
106	
107	Let `k̄` be an algebraically closed field. Let `X` be a nonsingular variety
108	over `k̄` (smooth, geometrically irreducible, separated, locally of finite
109	type, integral, reduced). Let `A` be an abelian variety over `k̄`
110	(a group object that is proper, smooth, and geometrically irreducible). For
111	every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` there is a *unique* regular morphism
112	`g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
113	
114	Milne's argument (`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1.4, Theorem 3.2):
115	let `U ⊆ X` be a maximal open of definition of `f` and `Z := X ∖ U`.
116	By the codim-$\geq 2$ extension theorem (Theorem 3.1, project-side
117	`thm:codim_one_extension`) the closed subset `Z` has codimension `≥ 2`.
118	By the pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy lemma for maps into a group variety
119	(Lemma 3.3, project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`), `Z` is either
120	empty or of pure codimension `1`. Both conditions force `Z = ∅`, so `U = X`
121	and `f` admits a regular representative on all of `X`. Uniqueness is the
122	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle (cf.
123	`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two regular morphisms `X ⟶ A` that
124	agree on a dense open of `X` agree everywhere.
125	
126	Blueprint reference: `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Milne, *Abelian
127	Varieties*, Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17). The pin in the chapter is at
128	`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50.
129	
130	iter-176+ proof skeleton:
131	1. Pick a `PartialMap` representative `φ : X.PartialMap A` of `f` (using
132	   `Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`, which exists for `X` reduced and `A`
133	   separated; both hold from the abelian-variety + variety instances).
134	2. Show `φ.domain = ⊤` by combining `thm:codim_one_extension` (codim `≥ 2`
135	   complement) with `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (complement empty or
136	   pure codim `1`).
137	3. Read off `g := φ.hom` (the morphism on the full domain) and verify
138	   `g.toRationalMap = f`.
139	4. Uniqueness: two `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ A` with `g₁.toRationalMap = g₂.toRationalMap`
140	   agree on a dense open of `X`, so by
141	   `AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` and reducedness of `X` they agree
142	   everywhere. -/
143	
144	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
145	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
146	
147	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
148	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
149	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
150	
151	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
152	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
153	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
154	`J → A`.
155	
156	iter-176+: body combines `thm:codim_one_extension` (A.4.a; codim-$\geq 2$
157	extension to a normal variety target — here the abelian variety, which is
158	complete) with `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (A.4.a; pure-codim-$1$
159	indeterminacy for maps into a group variety). The two combine to force the
160	indeterminacy locus empty; uniqueness is the standard agreement principle
161	for reduced `X` and separated `A`. -/
162	theorem extend_to_av
163	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
164	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
165	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
166	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
167	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
168	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
169	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
170	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
171	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
172	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
173	  sorry
174	
175	end RationalMap
176	
177	end Scheme
178	
179	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
64	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
65	  [IsProper C.hom]
66	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
67	
68	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
69	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
70	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
71	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
72	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
73	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
74	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
75	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
76	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
77	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g
78	
79	namespace IsAlbanese
80	
81	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
82	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
83	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
84	  Classical.choose h
85	
86	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
87	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
88	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
89	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
90	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
91	
92	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
95	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
96	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
97	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
98	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
99	
100	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
101	universal morphisms. -/
102	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
103	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
104	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
105	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
106	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
107	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
108	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
109	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
110	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
111	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
112	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
113	    simp
114	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
115	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
116	    change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
117	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
118	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
119	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
120	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
121	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
122	    simp
123	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
124	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
125	    change h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
126	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
127	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
128	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩
129	
130	end IsAlbanese
131	
132	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
133	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
134	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
135	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) where
136	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by
137	    intro K _ y Z fst snd h
138	    haveI : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
139	    exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))
140	      (asIso snd).symm inferInstance
141	
142	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
143	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
144	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
145	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
146	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
147	
148	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
149	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
150	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
151	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
152	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
153	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
154	
155	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
156	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
157	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
158	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
159	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
160	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
161	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
162	  grpObj : GrpObj J
163	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
164	  proper : IsProper J.hom
165	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
166	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
167	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
168	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
169	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
170	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
171	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
172	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
173	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
174	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
175	
176	/-- Genus-`0` Albanese witness. The witness object is the trivial terminal scheme
177	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec k))`; the `JacobianWitness` structural fields (proper, smooth,
178	geometrically irreducible, genus-stable, group-object) reduce to the
179	corresponding trivialities on `Spec k` via the structural cluster
180	`(𝟙_).hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)`. The `isAlbaneseFor` field consumes the **genus-0
181	abelian-variety rigidity** statement: for every pointed `(C, P)` and every
182	target group scheme `(A, η[A])`, the genus-0 hypothesis forces a morphism
183	`f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]` to equal `toUnit C ≫ η[A]`. Over an
184	algebraically closed base `k̄` this is
185	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` (in
186	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`, char-free, route-C, iter-163).
187	For a general `[Field k]` base the keystone is consumed via pullback to
188	`Spec k̄` + descent of the morphism equality along the faithfully-flat
189	surjection `Spec k̄ → Spec k`. The uniqueness clause cancels the epimorphism
190	`toUnit C` via `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective` (smooth ⟹ flat,
191	geometrically irreducible ⟹ surjective on `Spec k`).
192	
193	**Status (iter-172):** the `key` body remains `sorry`; the rigidity consumer
194	exists (declared) but still propagates upstream sorries from the iter-171
195	body skeleton in `Genus0BaseObjects.lean`. Wiring closes once
196	(i) the `Genus0BaseObjects` body-skeleton internal sorries discharge, and
197	(ii) the `k → k̄` pullback / descent step is filled. -/
198	noncomputable def genusZeroWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
199	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
200	    (h : genus C = 0) :
201	    JacobianWitness C where
202	  J := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))
203	  grpObj := inferInstance
204	  -- `(𝟙_).hom` is defeq `𝟙 (Spec k)`, but instance search does not unfold it;
205	  -- supply each instance for the identity and let term-elaboration close by defeq.
206	  proper := (inferInstance : IsProper (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
207	  smooth := (inferInstance : Smooth (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
208	  geomIrred := geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec k
209	  smoothGenus := by
210	    rw [h]; exact (inferInstance : SmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
211	  isAlbaneseFor := by
212	    -- For every `k`-rational marked point `P`, exhibit the Albanese datum for
213	    -- the pointed curve `(C, P)` with `J = 𝟙_` (blueprint C.2 / C.3).
214	    intro P
215	    -- The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map `toUnit C`.
216	    refine ⟨toUnit C, ?_, ?_⟩
217	    · -- Pointed condition: both `P ≫ toUnit C` and `η[𝟙_]` are maps `𝟙_ ⟶ 𝟙_`,
218	      -- equal by uniqueness of morphisms into the terminal object.
219	      exact toUnit_unique _ _
220	    · -- Universal factorisation: given `f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]`, the
221	      -- factoring morphism `𝟙_ ⟶ A` is the identity element `η[A]`.
222	      intro A _ _ _ _ f _hf
223	      -- Genus-0 RIGIDITY (C.2): `f` equals the constant morphism at the identity.
224	      -- The route-C content `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` (declared in
225	      -- `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`) is char-free and import-clean
226	      -- (proven iter-163 onward). To consume it for the general `[Field k]` goal,
227	      -- this body needs to pull back `(C, A, f, P)` along an algebraic closure
228	      -- `Spec k̄ → Spec k`, apply `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` in the
229	      -- pulled-back setting, then descend the morphism equality along the
230	      -- faithfully-flat surjection (the descent step itself is
231	      -- `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`, already used below to cancel the
232	      -- terminal epimorphism). The pullback functor + the descent of a morphism
233	      -- equality is the residual sub-build; it is real but is not gated on
234	      -- any out-of-file plan-level decision.
235	      have key : f = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
236	        sorry
237	      refine ⟨η[A], key, ?_⟩
238	      -- Uniqueness: any `g'` with `f = toUnit C ≫ g'` equals `η[A]`, by cancelling
239	      -- the epimorphism `toUnit C`. Its underlying scheme morphism is `C.hom`
240	      -- (`Over.toUnit_left`), which is faithfully flat (smooth ⟹ flat) and
241	      -- surjective (a geometrically irreducible curve over a field is nonempty and
242	      -- `Spec k` is a single point), hence an epimorphism (`Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`).
243	      intro g' hg'
244	      have hcancel : toUnit C ≫ g' = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by rw [← hg', ← key]
245	      have hepiL : Epi (toUnit C).left := by
246	        rw [Over.toUnit_left]; exact Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective C.hom
247	      haveI : Epi (toUnit C) := Over.epi_of_epi_left _
248	      exact (cancel_epi (toUnit C)).mp hcancel
249	
250	/-- The Albanese witness for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C`
251	of positive genus `g ≥ 1` over `k`. The underlying scheme is the Albanese
252	variety of `C` — classically the connected component of the identity of
253	`Pic_{C/k}`, or equivalently the Stein factorisation of the Abel–Jacobi
254	morphism on `Sym^g(C)`. Both constructions require infrastructure not yet
255	in Mathlib (FGA representability of the Picard functor for Route A;
256	symmetric powers + finite-group quotients + Stein factorisation +
257	Brill–Noether–Riemann–Roch for Route B); the iter-123 M3 route audit
258	(`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`) records both at midpoint ~6500 and
259	~9000 LOC respectively.
260	
261	**Status**: iter-134 scaffold — body is `sorry`. The body closure is M3
262	work, currently OFF-CRITICAL-PATH until M2 closes (per STRATEGY.md
263	§ M3, user-escalation-pending).
264	
265	**Iter-135**: the genus-stratified body restructure of
266	`nonempty_jacobianWitness` is now in place (Change 2.1 of iter-135
267	refactor); this scaffold is the positive-genus arm. Body closure
268	remains M3 work (off-critical-path; user-escalation-pending per
269	`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`). -/
270	noncomputable def positiveGenusWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
271	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
272	    (hg : 0 < genus C) :
273	    JacobianWitness C :=
274	  sorry
275	
276	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
277	curve. This is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C Jacobian
278	scaffolding (the other being `genusZeroWitness`); both are scheduled for body
279	closure post M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. It packages all five protected sorries
280	(`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`,
281	`instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records
282	that the Albanese property of the underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every
283	choice of $k$-rational marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor`
284	field of `JacobianWitness`).
285	
286	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
287	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
288	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
289	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
290	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
291	existence is assumed.
292	
293	**Iter-135 body restructure**: the body is now a `by_cases h :
294	genus C = 0` decomposition consuming `genusZeroWitness` (genus-0 arm)
295	and `positiveGenusWitness` (positive-genus arm). Both arms' bodies
296	remain `sorry` (M2.b body closure iter-138+; M3 body closure
297	off-critical-path); the restructure converts the inline `sorry` here
298	into honest delegation to the two scaffolds, removing one inline-`sorry`
299	site without prejudging either arm's body closure. -/
300	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
301	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
302	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := by
303	  by_cases h : genus C = 0
304	  · exact ⟨genusZeroWitness C h⟩
305	  · exact ⟨positiveGenusWitness C (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h)⟩
306	
307	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
308	Used to define `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of the four protected instances
309	on it. -/
310	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
311	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
312	    JacobianWitness C :=
313	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
314	
315	-- data
316	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
317	
318	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
319	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
320	witness is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C scaffolding
321	(the other being `genusZeroWitness`), both scheduled for body closure post
322	M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the
323	witness — a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of
324	relative dimension `0` is `Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is
325	absorbed into `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
326	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
327	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
328	  (jacobianWitness C).J
329	
330	namespace Jacobian
331	
332	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
333	
334	-- data
335	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
336	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
337	
338	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
339	genus of `C`. -/
340	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
341	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
342	
343	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
344	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
345	
346	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
347	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
348	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
349	
350	end Jacobian
351	
352	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	/-!
11	# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly
12	
13	This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
14	route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
15	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
16	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
17	tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
18	
19	The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
20	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
22	chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.
23	
24	The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:
25	
26	1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
27	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
28	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
29	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
30	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
31	2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
32	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
33	3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
34	
35	The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
36	canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
37	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.
38	
39	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
40	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
41	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
42	
43	## Encoding notes
44	
45	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
46	abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
47	relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
48	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
49	The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
50	may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to
51	`rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`, because it is the exact signature the
52	consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false

57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
64	
65	/-! ### Iter-167 dominance bridge for the canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` map
66	
67	The four product / Proj instances `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux` needs
68	(`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`, `IsReduced` of the product, and
69	`IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`) all ship from Lane A
70	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) as the instances `projGm_geomIrred`,
71	`projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, `projGm_isReduced`, and `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`. The
72	helper resolves them all by `infer_instance`.
73	
74	The one remaining bridge — dominance of the canonical inclusion `ι : Gm ⟶ ℙ¹` — is
75	file-local because its proof depends on the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1` (still
76	a Lane A scaffold sorry through iter-167). It is exposed here as a named top-level lemma
77	`iotaGm_isDominant` so the helper carries no inline `sorry`. -/
78	
79	/-- **The canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion `ι : Gm ⟶ ℙ¹` is dominant.** Here `ι` is the
80	"specialise the scaling action at `x = 1`" map `lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ gmScalingP1`.
81	Once Lane A ships the concrete `gmScalingP1` body (chartwise glue), this becomes the standard
82	open immersion `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] ↪ ℙ¹` (sending `λ` to `[λ : 1]`), which is dense in the
83	irreducible `ℙ¹`.
84	
85	Project-side bridge pending Lane A's concrete `gmScalingP1` body. -/
86	private lemma iotaGm_isDominant [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
87	    IsDominant (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
88	      ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar).left :=
89	  sorry
90	
91	/-- **Helper (pointed case): a morphism `f : ℙ¹ → A` killing `0 ∈ ℙ¹` is the constant `1`.**
92	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, if `f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A` satisfies
93	`ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]`, then `f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)`
94	(equivalently `f = toUnit ProjectiveLineBar ≫ η[A]`).
95	
96	This is the pointed core of the `𝔾ₘ`-scaling shortcut: form `h := gmScalingP1 ≫ f`, feed it
97	to Cor 1.5 (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) with `V = ProjectiveLineBar`, `W = Gm`, base
98	points `zeroPt`, `onePt`. The `W`-axis collapses by `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, leaving
99	`h = fst ≫ fV` (the relation `f(λx) = fV(x)`). Specialising at `x = 1` (via the canonical
100	inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` given by `λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ`) and using density of `Gm ⊆ ℙ¹` plus
101	separatedness of `A` (via `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`, the same Mathlib bridge
102	`rigidity_core` uses inline), we conclude `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ (onePt ≫ fV)`. The basepoint
103	hypothesis then pins `onePt ≫ fV = η[A]`.
104	
105	**Status (iter-167):** body fully refactored — all five in-line product/Proj `sorry`s have
106	been eliminated. Four of them (`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`, `IsReduced`
107	of the product, and `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`) ship from Lane A
108	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` instances `projGm_geomIrred`,
109	`projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, `projGm_isReduced`, `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`) and resolve
110	by `infer_instance` in scope. The fifth — dominance of the canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` map — is
111	exposed as the named top-level bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` above (a single `sorry` pending
112	Lane A's `gmScalingP1` body). The helper itself contains no `sorry`. -/
113	private theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
114	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
115	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
116	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
117	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)
118	    (hf0 : ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]) :
119	    f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
120	  -- W-axis collapse via `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, precomposed with `onePt`:
121	  -- `lift zeroPt onePt ≫ gmScalingP1 = zeroPt`.
122	  have hcollapse :
123	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar) ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar
124	        = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
125	    have hbase := gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero kbar
126	    -- Rewrite `lift zeroPt onePt = onePt ≫ lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm)`.
127	    have hreshape :
128	        lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
129	          = Gm.onePt kbar ≫
130	            lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) := by
131	      rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id]
132	      congr 1
133	      rw [← Category.assoc,
134	        toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
135	    rw [hreshape, Category.assoc, hbase, ← Category.assoc,
136	      toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
137	  -- The Cor 1.5 basepoint hypothesis `lift v₀ w₀ ≫ (σ ≫ f) = η[A]`.
138	  have hcorhyp :
139	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
140	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = η[A] := by
141	    rw [← Category.assoc, hcollapse]; exact hf0
142	  -- Apply Cor 1.5.
143	  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
144	    (V := ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (W := Gm kbar)
145	    (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
146	    (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) hcorhyp
147	  -- W-axis restriction: `lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ σ ≫ f = (1 : Gm ⟶ A)`.
148	  have hfW :
149	      lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
150	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = (1 : Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
151	    rw [← Category.assoc, gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero, Category.assoc, hf0]
152	    exact Hom.one_def.symm
153	  -- `snd ≫ fW = snd ≫ 1 = 1` (using `toUnit` uniqueness).
154	  have hSndFW :
155	      snd (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)
156	        ≫ (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
157	          ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f))
158	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
159	    rw [hfW, Hom.one_def, ← Category.assoc]
160	    congr 1
161	    exact toUnit_unique _ _
162	  -- `key` now reads `σ ≫ f = fst ≫ fV` (after collapsing the W-axis to 1).
163	  rw [hSndFW, _root_.mul_one] at key
164	  -- Name the V-axis restriction `fV`.
165	  set fV : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A :=
166	    lift (𝟙 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ Gm.onePt kbar)
167	      ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) with hfVdef
168	  -- Precompose `key` with `gmInP1 := lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm) : Gm → ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm`
169	  -- ("λ ↦ (1, λ)"). The resulting morphism `ι := gmInP1 ≫ σ : Gm → ℙ¹` is the canonical
170	  -- inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` ("λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ"); on its image, `f` is constant at `onePt ≫ fV`.
171	  set iotaGm : Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
172	    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
173	      ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar with hιdef
174	  have hιf :
175	      iotaGm ≫ f = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
176	    calc iotaGm ≫ f
177	        = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
178	            ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) := by rw [hιdef, Category.assoc]
179	      _ = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
180	            ≫ (fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar) ≫ fV) := by rw [key]
181	      _ = (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
182	            ≫ fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)) ≫ fV := by
183	          rw [Category.assoc]
184	      _ = (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) ≫ fV := by rw [lift_fst]
185	      _ = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
186	          rw [Category.assoc]
187	  -- Separatedness of the target `A` over `Spec k̄` in `OverClass.fromOver` form.
188	  haveI hAsep : IsSeparated A.hom := IsProper.toIsSeparated
189	  haveI : IsSeparated (A.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := hAsep
190	  -- `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` and the product geom-irred / LOFT / IsReduced
191	  -- instances are all auto-resolved via Lane A's exports in `Genus0BaseObjects`
192	  -- (`projectiveLineBar_isReduced`, `projGm_geomIrred`, `projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`,
193	  -- `projGm_isReduced`). Dominance of `ι.left` is the only file-local bridge, cited
194	  -- explicitly via `iotaGm_isDominant` (the `set`-binding `hιdef` exposes `iotaGm.left`
195	  -- definitionally as `(lift _ _ ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar).left`).
196	  haveI hιDom : IsDominant iotaGm.left := iotaGm_isDominant
197	  -- Globalise: `ι ≫ f = ι ≫ (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ (onePt ≫ fV))` gives, by dominance, `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ c`.
198	  have hf_eq :
199	      f = toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
200	    -- Promote the Over morphism equality from the underlying scheme equality.
201	    have hgoal :
202	        iotaGm ≫ f
203	          = iotaGm ≫ (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
204	              (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV)) := by
205	      have hreshape :
206	          iotaGm ≫ (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
207	              (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV))
208	            = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
209	        rw [← Category.assoc,
210	          toUnit_unique (iotaGm ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (Gm kbar))]
211	      rw [hreshape, hιf]
212	    refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
213	    refine ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of kbar))
214	      (X := (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left) (Y := A.left)
215	      (f := f.left)
216	      (g := (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
217	        (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV)).left) iotaGm.left ?_
218	    rw [← Over.comp_left, ← Over.comp_left]
219	    exact congrArg Over.Hom.left hgoal
220	  -- Pin `onePt ≫ fV = η[A]` via the basepoint hypothesis `hf0`.
221	  have hc : ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV = η[A] := by
222	    have hcomp := hf0
223	    rw [hf_eq] at hcomp
224	    rw [← Category.assoc,
225	      toUnit_unique (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar))
226	        (𝟙 _),
227	      Category.id_comp] at hcomp
228	    exact hcomp
229	  rw [hf_eq, hc, ← Hom.one_def]
230	
231	/-- **A morphism `ℙ¹ → A` is constant.** Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
232	morphism `f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A` from the projective line into an abelian variety
233	`A` (a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme) is constant: it factors through
234	a single `k̄`-point `a₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ A`, i.e. `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀`.
235	
236	The single-curve base case of Milne's Proposition 3.10. **Route resolved iter-164: the
237	𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut** — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`,
238	char-general. The proof body proceeds by translating in the group `A` to reduce to the
239	basepoint case `zeroPt ≫ f = η[A]` (handled by the helper
240	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`), then un-translating.
241	
242	See blueprint `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`
243	(Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10).
244	
245	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via the helper's residuals
246	(three product-instance Mathlib bridges + dominance of the canonical `Gm → ℙ¹` map). Lifts
247	to axiom-clean once those are discharged. -/
248	theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const
249	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
250	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
251	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
252	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) :
253	    ∃ a₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ A,
254	      f = toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ a₀ := by
255	  -- Witness: `a₀ := zeroPt ≫ f` (the value of `f` at the scaling fixed point `0 ∈ ℙ¹`).
256	  refine ⟨ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f, ?_⟩
257	  -- Translate: form `f' := f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀)` and apply the helper to `f'`.
258	  have hf' :
259	      (f / (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f))
260	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
261	    apply morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
262	    -- Show `zeroPt ≫ (f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀)) = η[A]`.
263	    rw [GrpObj.comp_div]
264	    have hv :
265	        ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫
266	            toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
267	              (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f)
268	          = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f := by
269	      rw [← Category.assoc,
270	        toUnit_unique (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar))
271	          (𝟙 _),
272	        Category.id_comp]
273	    rw [hv, div_self', Hom.one_def, toUnit_unique (toUnit _) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
274	  -- Untranslate: `f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀) = 1` ⟺ `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀`.
275	  exact div_eq_one.mp hf'
276	
277	/-- **A genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`.** Over an algebraically closed field
278	`k̄`, a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` with `genus C = 0` is isomorphic — in
279	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))` — to the concrete projective line `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`.
280	
281	Hartshorne's Example IV.1.3.5 (Riemann–Roch). Its formalisation is a genuine sub-build:
282	Mathlib has no Riemann–Roch for curves; this is the dominant long pole flagged by the
283	iter-164 progress-critic.
284	
285	See blueprint `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
286	(Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
287	
288	**Status (iter-166):** signature refactored to the concrete `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`; body
289	remains `sorry` (RR bridge — iter-167+). -/
290	theorem genusZero_curve_iso_P1
291	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
292	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
293	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
294	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0) :
295	    Nonempty (C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :=
296	  sorry
297	
298	/-- **Headline: rigidity for a pointed genus-`0` curve.** Over an algebraically closed field
299	`k̄` (arbitrary characteristic — no `[CharZero kbar]`), let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically
300	irreducible curve with `genus C = 0` and `A` an abelian variety (smooth proper geom-irred group
301	scheme). Then every morphism `f : C ⟶ A` killing a `k̄`-point `p` (`p ≫ f = η[A]`) equals the
302	constant morphism at the identity, `f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
303	
304	This is the project's committed characteristic-free statement that `genusZeroWitness` consumes
305	(via the `k̄ → k` descent step hosted in `AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian`). Its signature mirrors
306	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` **verbatim except**
307	the `[CharZero kbar]` instance is dropped. Combine `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (`C ≅ ℙ¹`) with
308	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (`ℙ¹ → A` constant) and pin the constant value to `η[A]` via
309	the pointed hypothesis. No `df = 0`, no Serre duality, no Picard representability.
310	
311	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
312	(RR bridge, iter-167+) and `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`'s helper residuals. Once the RR
313	bridge closes and the helper's product-instance + dominance sorries discharge, lifts to
314	axiom-clean. -/
315	theorem rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme
316	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
317	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
318	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
319	    [IsProper C.hom]
320	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
321	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
322	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
323	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
324	    (f : C ⟶ A)
325	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
326	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
327	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) := by
328	  -- Transport `f` along `C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar` to a morphism `g : ℙ¹ ⟶ A`.
329	  obtain ⟨φ⟩ := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus
330	  set g : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A := φ.inv ≫ f with hgdef
331	  -- `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` gives `g = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀` for some `a₀`.
332	  obtain ⟨a₀, hga₀⟩ := morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const g
333	  -- Pin `a₀ = η[A]` via the pointed hypothesis on `f`.
334	  have hpoint : (p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ g = η[A] := by
335	    rw [hgdef, Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc φ.hom, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
336	    exact _hf
337	  have hcst : a₀ = η[A] := by
338	    rw [hga₀, ← Category.assoc,
339	      toUnit_unique ((p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (𝟙 _),
340	      Category.id_comp] at hpoint
341	    exact hpoint
342	  rw [hcst] at hga₀
343	  -- Back-transport: `f = φ.hom ≫ g = φ.hom ≫ toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ η[A] = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
344	  calc f
345	      = φ.hom ≫ φ.inv ≫ f := by
346	        rw [← Category.assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
347	    _ = φ.hom ≫ g := by rw [hgdef]
348	    _ = φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ η[A] := by rw [hga₀]
349	    _ = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
350	        rw [← Category.assoc,
351	          toUnit_unique (φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit C)]
352	
353	
354	end AlgebraicGeometry
355

2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.

4	Authors: Christian Merten
